is-odd@0.1.2._note: Registry-sourced is-odd, distinct from the git-sourced is-odd@3.0.1 declared at root. Pulled in by is-even@1.0.0 (github-shorthand dep) which declares is-odd@^0.1.2.

warnings: PRIMARY ASSERTION: Both is-odd (full git+https URL) and is-even (github: shorthand) MUST report source='git'. If either reports source='registry', Mend has failed to detect the git source type for that manifest form.

SHORTHAND EXPANSION: The manifest value 'github:jonschlinkert/is-even#1.0.0' is syntactic sugar for 'git+https://github.com/jonschlinkert/is-even.git#1.0.0'. Mend must expand the github: shorthand before source classification. Failure mode: source reported as 'registry' or 'unknown' instead of 'git'.

DUAL is-odd: Two distinct is-odd entries coexist — is-odd@3.0.1 (git-sourced, root direct dep) and is-odd@0.1.2 (registry-sourced, transitive via is-even). Mend must track both and not collapse them. If only one appears, the other was dropped.

TRANSITIVE SOURCE: is-number@6.0.0 is a registry-sourced transitive of the git-sourced is-odd@3.0.1. Mend must follow transitive edges from git-sourced packages into registry packages. If is-number is missing, the transitive edge from a git dep is not being walked.

MEND LIMITATION: Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver. The npm-resolver fallback may misparse the 'git' descriptor block inside bun.lock package tuples, silently dropping source_detail or reclassifying source as 'registry'. This probe is an exploratory/regression probe for that gap.
SHA1: Git-sourced packages have no registry URL and no tgz SHA1. Mend must handle absent SHA1 for git deps gracefully (emit null or omit) — a hard failure here means git deps are blocked entirely.
